messages.content:
hello i am currently developing an app named Clear Up where it is suppose to detect written text or text in the camera just like the google lens and this app is design to help people with dyslexia. so basically the text will be detected and then it will say the word using text to speech feature but i am no longer near there because i am stuck here in making a simple button that activates the camera.
after running the program in my phone it just doesnt work and i think its in my MainActivity.kt that is wrong because i noticed that when running my main_activity.xml it just says Hello Android when i did not configured the MainActivity.kt yet. just provide me the explanations and when i ask you to give me or revise the code just do what i tell you okay? because im sure its gonna crash this site when too much token. 

IMPORTANT!!

anyway when i try to run the app in my phone the Clear up has stopped working disappears and all i see is a white solid color, i wanted the @layout/main_activity to show when i open the program.

<?xml version="1.0" encoding="utf-8"?>
<LinearLayout xmlns:android="http://schemas.android.com/apk/res/android"
    android:orientation="vertical"
    android:layout_width="fill_parent"
    android:layout_height="fill_parent"
    android:paddingLeft="20dp"
    android:paddingRight="20dp">

    <FrameLayout
        android:id="@+id/camera_preview"
        android:layout_width="match_parent"
        android:layout_height="200dp"
        android:layout_marginTop="20dp"
        android:scaleType="centerCrop"
        android:background="@drawable/image_outline" />

    <Button
        android:id="@+id/image_capture"
        android:layout_width="wrap_content"
        android:layout_height="wrap_content"
        android:layout_margin="20dp"
        android:background="@drawable/button_background"
        android:text="@string/capture_button"
        android:textColor="@color/purple_200"
        android:gravity="center"
        android:textSize="25sp" />

</LinearLayout>

If your app displays a solid white color and not the expected layout on launch, there could be several reasons behind this issue. Let's troubleshoot this step by step:

### Check Layout File Name
First, ensure that the layout file name you mentioned (`main_activity.xml`) matches exactly with what you are using in the `setContentView()` method call in your `CameraActivity`'s `onCreate()` method. The line should look like this:

```java
setContentView(R.layout.main_activity);
```

### Verify Activity Registration
Ensure that `CameraActivity` is correctly registered in your `AndroidManifest.xml` file and is set as the launcher activity if it is supposed to be the entry point of your app. Here is how you might register the activity:

```xml
<application
    ...>
    <activity android:name=".CameraActivity">
        <intent-filter>
            <action android:name="android.intent.action.MAIN"/>

            <category android:name="android.intent.category.LAUNCHER"/>
        </intent-filter>
    </activity>
    <!-- Other activities -->
</application>
```

### Review Resources
Make sure that all resources referenced in your layout file are available and correctly named in your resources folder. For example, check the following:

- `@drawable/image_outline` exists in the `drawable` folder.
- `@drawable/button_background` exists in the `drawable` folder.
- `@string/capture_button` is defined inside your `strings.xml` file.
- `@color/purple_200` is defined inside your `colors.xml` file.

### Error in Code
Review your `CameraActivity` for any runtime errors that might be causing the app to stop or not display the contents correctly. This includes checking the `Camera` initialization, the `CameraPreview` setup, and any other logic that runs during the activity startup.

### Check Logcat
Run the app with Android Studio's Logcat window open. Look for any exceptions or error messages that occur when the app launches. These messages can provide valuable hints as to what might be going wrong.

### Ensuring Camera Initialization
Make sure the `Camera` object is being initialized properly. If there is an issue with accessing the camera, it might prevent your app from displaying the expected UI. This could be due to missing permissions or the camera being used by another application. Remember, you need to request camera permission at runtime for apps targeting Android 6.0 (API level 23) and above:

```xml
<uses-permission android:name="android.permission.CAMERA"/>
```

And in your activity, check for permission:

```java
if (ContextCompat.checkSelfPermission(this, Manifest.permission.CAMERA)
        != PackageManager.PERMISSION_GRANTED) {
    ActivityCompat.requestPermissions(this, new String[]{Manifest.permission.CAMERA}, PERMISSION_REQUEST_CAMERA);
    // Callback is managed in onRequestPermissionsResult
} else {
    // Permission has already been granted, initialize the camera
}
```

By following these steps, you should be able to identify and resolve the issue preventing your layout from displaying correctly.
i forgot that i have a button in my xml file. can you make it whenever i click the button it the app will ask to grant camera permission?
